disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A 5-day-old neonate has become progressively difficult to rouse and is now barely responsive, over roughly 12 hours
trigger_question_examples: How is he responding to you right now?
Is he waking for feeds or hard to rouse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's hardly responding now — we can barely wake him and he won't feed at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abnormal movements and posturing — pedalling/bicycling leg movements and rigid stretched-out (fencing) posturing
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the movements you are seeing?
Is his body going stiff or floppy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His legs pedal in the air like he's riding a bike, and his body goes rigid and stretched out; other times he's completely floppy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A distinctive sweet, maple-syrup / burnt-sugar odour to the urine and cerumen
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any unusual smell?
Does his nappy or skin smell different?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — his wee and even his earwax smell really sweet, like maple syrup or burnt sugar. It's quite strong.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fast, deep, laboured breathing in the neonate
trigger_question_examples: Is he breathing fast or working hard to breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His breathing is fast and looks heavy and deep, more than normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor feeding, vomiting and failure to gain weight since birth, on exclusive standard (high-protein) formula
trigger_question_examples: How has his feeding been since he was born?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's been a poor feeder and vomiting since the first days, and he hasn't gained weight — he's on a standard formula, that's all he's had.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset of decline followed the introduction and continuation of protein feeds after an initially well period at birth
trigger_question_examples: When exactly did the changes start relative to feeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He seemed fine when he was born with good scores, and it's only over the last few days of feeding that he's gone downhill.
